No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to eradicating poverty or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to health, well-being, or related targets.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops relevant technical skills for employment in the AI and communication industries (Target 4.4).
evidence: 瞭解AI對傳播領域的影響 (Understand the impact of AI on the communication field)
發展個人的AI策略 (develop a personal AI strategy)

Gender Equality.reason: The course addresses 'AI bias' under ethics, which inherently includes gender bias. This aligns with eliminating discrimination and promoting gender equality through technology and policy (Targets 5.1, 5.c).

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course directly addresses AI's impact on automation, the content industry, and replacing existing jobs, aligning with technological upgrading (Target 8.2) and the future of decent work (Target 8.5).
evidence: AI與內容產業 (AI and content industry)
如何用AI來取代自己現有的工作？ (How to use AI to replace your own existing work?)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course fundamentally focuses on AI technologies, scientific research, and innovation (Target 9.5), specifically for upgrading the communication industry and enhancing ICT (Target 9.c).
evidence: AI與傳播科技應用 (AI in...Technologies, Communication and Implications)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course addresses 'AI bias' under ethics, which is crucial for ensuring equal opportunity and reducing inequalities of outcome by eliminating discriminatory practices (Target 10.3).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to urban development, housing, or infrastructure for communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to sustainable consumption, waste reduction, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or resilience.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course discusses 'data privacy' and 'AI bias' under ethics. Data privacy relates to fundamental freedoms (Target 16.10), and addressing AI bias supports non-discriminatory policies and accountable institutions (Target 16.b, 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course focuses on AI and communication technology, which are enabling ICTs (Target 17.8). Understanding and applying these technologies can enhance knowledge sharing for sustainable development (Target 17.6).
evidence: AI與傳播科技應用 (AI in...Technologies, Communication and Implications)
